methods.title: GRAFT-Net: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Network for Textual and Knowledge Base Question Answering
methods.notes: Graph expansion and graph-based evidence composition.

methods.title: PullNet: Open Domain Question Answering with Iterative Retrieval on Knowledge Bases and Text
methods.notes: Iteratively expands a question-specific graph.

methods.title: Multi-hop Reading Comprehension through Question Decomposition and Rescoring
methods.notes: Decomposes compositional questions into simpler subquestions and globally rescores decompositions.

methods.title: Dynamically Fused Graph Network for Multi-hop Reasoning
methods.notes: Builds and traverses an entity graph over scattered text evidence.

methods.title: Learning to Retrieve Reasoning Paths over Wikipedia Graph for Question Answering
methods.notes: Sequentially retrieves evidence paragraphs conditioned on earlier hops.

methods.title: Unsupervised Question Decomposition for Question Answering
methods.notes: Learns one-to-many decomposition without manually labeled decompositions and recomposes subanswers.

methods.title: Scalable Multi-Hop Relational Reasoning for Knowledge-Aware Question Answering
methods.notes: Performs multi-hop relational reasoning over extracted knowledge-graph subgraphs.

methods.title: Hierarchical Graph Network for Multi-hop Question Answering
methods.notes: Represents question paragraphs sentences and entities at multiple graph granularities.

methods.title: Leveraging Passage Retrieval with Generative Models for Open Domain Question Answering
methods.notes: Fusion-in-decoder reader over retrieved passages.

methods.title: Learning Dense Retrieval for Multi-hop Question Answering
methods.notes: Dense retriever trained for multi-hop evidence acquisition.

methods.title: Baleen: Robust Multi-Hop Reasoning at Scale via Condensed Retrieval
methods.notes: Condenses retrieved evidence between hops to control search and context growth.

methods.title: Decomposing Complex Questions Makes Multi-Hop QA Easier and More Interpretable
methods.notes: Uses relation extraction reader and comparison stages to form a decomposed evidence path.

methods.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
methods.notes: Interleaves natural-language reasoning traces with environment actions such as Wikipedia lookup.

methods.title: Interleaving Retrieval with Chain-of-Thought Reasoning for Knowledge-Intensive Multi-Step Questions
methods.notes: Interleaves generated reasoning with retrieval.

methods.title: Active Retrieval Augmented Generation
methods.notes: Retrieval is triggered by generation uncertainty; verify the precise multi-hop scope before using it for a focused comparison.

methods.title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve Generate and Critique through Self-Reflection
methods.notes: Joint retrieval generation and critique policy.

methods.title: RAPTOR: Recursive Abstractive Processing for Tree-Organized Retrieval
methods.notes: Changes retrieval granularity and context organization.

methods.title: Corrective Retrieval Augmented Generation
methods.notes: Evaluates retrieved context and may trigger web search plus decomposition and recomposition.

methods.title: Adaptive-RAG: Learning to Adapt Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models through Question Complexity
methods.notes: Selects no-retrieval single-step or iterative retrieval strategies from predicted query complexity.

methods.title: End-to-End Beam Retrieval for Multi-Hop Question Answering
methods.notes: Maintains multiple partial evidence hypotheses to reduce early-hop search errors.

methods.title: From Local to Global: A Graph RAG Approach to Query-Focused Summarization
methods.notes: Builds an entity graph and community summaries to expose local and global corpus evidence.

methods.title: HippoRAG: Neurobiologically Inspired Long-Term Memory for Large Language Models
methods.notes: Uses a graph-based memory index and personalized PageRank for efficient multi-hop retrieval.

methods.title: EfficientRAG: Efficient Retriever for Multi-Hop Question Answering
methods.notes: Decomposes a multi-hop question while pruning irrelevant subquestions and documents during retrieval.

benchmarks.name: HotpotQA
benchmarks.caveat: Supporting-fact annotations are a proxy rather than a complete latent chain.

benchmarks.name: 2WikiMultiHopQA
benchmarks.caveat: Construction and evidence provenance should be considered in cross-dataset comparisons.

benchmarks.name: HoVer
benchmarks.caveat: The claim-verification setting is not answer extraction and includes multi-sentence claims at higher hop counts.

benchmarks.name: KILT: a Benchmark for Knowledge Intensive Language Tasks
MuSiQue: Multihop Questions via Single-hop Question Composition
benchmarks.caveat: Reported metrics depend on the retrieval setting and distractor construction.

benchmarks.name: QASC
benchmarks.caveat: Two annotated facts do not eliminate the need for open-corpus retrieval or commonsense composition.

mappings.common_confounder: Incomplete graph coverage
Retriever and reader errors are coupled
Extra iterations change compute budget
Subquestion quality and rescoring are coupled
Graph construction quality confounds fusion quality
Path supervision and corpus graph quality are coupled
Subquestion fluency does not guarantee answerable decomposition
Subgraph extraction quality limits observed reasoning
Paragraph selection changes the reader input
Retrieved passages may already encode ranking gains
Gold evidence and corpus mismatch
Generated thoughts can leak answer priors

mappings.observable_diagnostic: retrieval timing and conditional answer quality
mappings.common_confounder: Uncertainty estimates and retrieval frequency are coupled
Self-critique is not a causal test
Granularity also changes retrieval recall
Graph construction quality confounds fusion quality
Path supervision and corpus graph quality are coupled
Paragraph selection changes the reader input
Condensation may alter both evidence exposure and retrieval query quality
Decomposition and comparison errors are coupled
Reasoning traces are not by themselves evidence of causal grounding
Web augmentation changes the underlying evidence corpus
Question complexity labels may encode dataset-specific difficulty

mappings.intervention: Beam search over partial evidence paths
mappings.common_confounder: Beam width changes both compute and the candidate pool
Graph construction and summary quality jointly affect evidence exposure
Entity extraction quality changes the graph and retrieval results
Pruning can trade recall for efficiency
